mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically expanding equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 6.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, directly boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 6.0% within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergent movements between revolving credit (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) signal fragmented aggregate demand, which increases earnings volatility and compresses equity risk premiums, generating negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS falls below 300000 and HOUST exceeds 1900
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of historical episodes, or the interaction term in a multivariate regression yields a p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) during late-cycle expansions reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, directly supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO rises for three consecutive months in a late-cycle environment.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0% confirms strong aggregate demand, leading to upward earnings revisions by analysts and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth surpasses 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with falling unemployment (UNRATE) signals a self-reinforcing labor expansion, which historically precedes sustained equity bull markets and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the cross-product term in a factor model shows no statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month CPI increases erode real consumer purchasing power and compress equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon regardless of other macro conditions.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of months where CPIAUCSL rises consecutively for two months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 0.5% alongside rising CPI, the Fed's tightening stance increases discount rates and suppresses risk appetite, driving negative S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL rise simultaneously above the specified thresholds.
